skill.name: firecrawl-scraper
skill.description: Extract web page content, capture screenshots, parse PDFs, and crawl websites through the Firecrawl API.
skill.summary: Use Firecrawl for web scraping, screenshots, PDF parsing, and website crawling.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found an external command, a hardcoded URL, and two weak cryptography alerts. The cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 34 are false positives because those lines contain description and troubleshooting text, not cryptographic code. The documented npx install command is a real supply-chain risk for a community skill, so publication should include a warning.

medium_findings.title: External Installer Command
medium_findings.description: The skill documentation instructs users to run an npx command that installs a community skill from an external source. This is not automatic execution by the skill, but it creates supply-chain risk because users may run remote installer code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly present in the installation section. It is legitimate installation guidance, but npx can retrieve and execute external package logic.

low_findings.title: External Repository Link
low_findings.description: The skill links to a GitHub repository for examples. This is a normal documentation pattern, but it sends users outside the audited skill content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is visible and only appears as a documentation link. No evidence found that the skill automatically sends data to this URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The installation section includes a shell command using npx. Users should review the target package and repository before running it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell command is clearly documented. The risk depends on user execution and the integrity of the external package source.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable extraction from pages, PDFs, and crawled websites. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Firecrawl for scraping, screenshots, and crawling tasks.
actual_capabilities: Guides deep content extraction from web pages using Firecrawl.
Supports workflows that require clicking, scrolling, or other page interaction.
Supports screenshot capture for web pages.
Supports PDF parsing through the Firecrawl API.
Supports batch scraping across multiple URLs.
Recommends configuring API keys through environment variables.
limitations: Requires a Firecrawl API key before use.
Depends on network access to the Firecrawl service.
Provides only a brief local guide and links elsewhere for examples.
Users must confirm that scraping is allowed for each target site.

use_cases.title: Collect Web Research
use_cases.description: Extract article, documentation, or page content for structured research summaries.

use_cases.title: Audit Website Content
use_cases.description: Crawl selected pages and capture screenshots to review site structure and page content.

use_cases.title: Process Source PDFs
use_cases.description: Use Firecrawl PDF parsing to convert linked documents into reviewable text.

prompt_templates.title: Scrape One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firecrawl-scraper skill to extract the main content from this URL and summarize the important points.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Page Evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl to capture a screenshot and extract visible page text from this URL for review.

prompt_templates.title: Parse Linked PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the firecrawl-scraper skill to parse this PDF URL and return the main sections, key facts, and missing text concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Crawl a Small Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl to crawl these URLs, extract page content, identify duplicate topics, and produce a concise site content inventory.

output_examples.output: A clean summary of the page purpose, core features, setup steps, and links that need follow-up.

output: A list of crawled pages grouped by topic.
Notes about missing pages, duplicate content, and pages that need manual review.

output_examples.output: A section-by-section digest with key findings, extracted facts, and warnings for unreadable or incomplete areas.

best_practices: Store the Firecrawl API key in environment variables.
Start with a small URL set before running broader crawls.
Check target site terms and rate limits before scraping.
anti_patterns: Do not scrape sites where access is prohibited.
Do not run the documented installer command without reviewing the source.
Do not assume screenshots or parsed PDFs capture every hidden element.

faq.question: What does this skill use Firecrawl for?
faq.answer: It uses Firecrawl for web page extraction, screenshots, PDF parsing, and website crawling workflows.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documentation says to configure a Firecrawl API key before use.

faq.question: Can it handle page interactions?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill says it is useful when clicking, scrolling, or similar page interaction is required.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because the documentation includes an external npx installer command.

faq.question: Were the weak cryptography alerts confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines contain plain description and troubleshooting text, not cryptographic algorithms.

faq.question: Does the local skill include detailed examples?
faq.answer: No. The local file links to an external GitHub repository for examples.
